threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know someone’s new when they ask how to get to Shoreditch and don’t realize it’s a twenty-minute walk, not a Tube ride.
Caught a glimpse of a fox casually trotting alongside the 133 last night. Only in Brixton do the street animals have that level of chill.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid rollerblading past a kebab shop, while an older couple shared a bottle of wine on the curb. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery, where murals change monthly. It's a feast for your eyes and stomach.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local rolls their eyes at tourists fumbling with the BART map—while they navigate the delays and hot brakes like it's a rite of passage.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the bus stop crowd with an ukulele while a rat scuttled past. Only in the Tenderloin does the Muni stop feel like a mini show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all Gràcia bars close at 2 AM, but many stay open until dawn—just depends which ones. The locals know the good spots.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their excitement at ordering 'pan con tomate' but still pronouncing it wrong, like 'tomato bread' in English.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of just 'public transport' while waving your contactless like it's a magic wand.
Last night on the 36, a couple argued over a missing shoe—turns out it was under the seat the whole time. Classic Peckham chaos.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a guy selling fresh falafel from a bike cart while a street artist painted a mural of a cat on a nearby wall. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop that serves beer brewed on-site. Nothing like a late-night döner with a local brew to end the night!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their knee-jerk reaction to BART delays—rolling eyes and a resigned sigh. Tourists just look confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy singing opera on the 31 and the whole bus just went quiet. Two stops later, he had the entire bus clapping. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every plaza is for gathering, but locals know the quietest spots behind the trees are where the real conversations happen.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still hesitate at street crossings, waiting for the green man even when traffic is sparse.
